title: Signed and unsigned types should not be mixed in expressions
description: Signed and unsigned types should not be mixed in expressionsAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misracertWhy is this an issue?More InfoSome signed to unsigned conversions may lead to implementation-defined behavior. This behavior may not be consistent with developer expectations.If you need to mix signed and unsigned types, you should make your intent explicit by using explicit casts and avoiding implicit casts.This rule will detect implicit conversions that change the signedness.Noncompliant code examplevoid f(int a) { unsigned int b = a; // Noncompliant int c = (a > 0) ? a : b; // Noncompliant if (a > b) { // Noncompliant // ... } }Compliant solutionvoid f(int a) { unsigned int b = static_cast<unsigned int>(a); // Compliant }Available In:Catch issues
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Signed and unsigned types should not be mixed in expressions. Use explicit casts to clarify intent.
